description:
You are a compliance officer who has been asked to perform a very small-scale audit of recent SSH activity.  
    The company already collected raw data in a single text file:

        /home/user/compliance/logs/audit.log

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Read / parse the log file.  
    2. Calculate the following four (4) items, **using only the entries whose “RESULT” field equals “FAIL”**:  
       • Total number of failed SSH login attempts.  
       • Total number of unique IP addresses that produced at least one failure.  
       • The IP address that generated the largest number of failures and its exact failure count.  
       • A timestamp showing when you generated the report (UTC, ISO-8601 format).  
    3. Write a plain-text compliance report to  

        /home/user/compliance/reports/ssh_audit_report.txt  

    Required report format (exact, including the colon, spaces and field order ‑ each item one per line):  

        TotalFailedSSHAttempts: <integer>
        UniqueOffendingIPs: <integer>
        MostCommonOffender: <ip> (<integer> attempts)
        ReportGeneratedOn: <YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ>

       • All angle-bracket placeholders (“<…>”) above must be replaced by the computed values.  
       • The timestamp must be in UTC and match the ISO-8601 pattern shown (`Z` suffix).  
       • No extra blank lines, no additional text.  

    4. Display the same four lines on STDOUT so the auditor instantly sees the result in the terminal.

    Notes & constraints  
    • You have normal user privileges only; do not use sudo.  
    • If the destination directory (/home/user/compliance/reports) does not exist, create it.  
    • The entire task should take no more than three or four typical shell commands (e.g., grep, awk, sort, uniq, wc, date, mkdir, redirect, etc.).  
    • The automated grader will check the exact file path and exact line-by-line content of ssh_audit_report.txt, as well as confirm that the four lines were printed to STDOUT.
truth:
Initial file hierarchy that exists before the agent starts:

        /home/user/compliance/
            └── logs/
                └── audit.log

    Contents of /home/user/compliance/logs/audit.log (11 lines total):

        2023-10-01T09:15:21Z IP=192.168.1.45 USER=root RESULT=FAIL
        2023-10-01T09:15:23Z IP=192.168.1.45 USER=root RESULT=FAIL
        2023-10-01T10:05:11Z IP=10.0.0.12 USER=admin RESULT=FAIL
        2023-10-02T08:22:04Z IP=172.16.0.4 USER=admin RESULT=SUCCESS
        2023-10-03T11:01:45Z IP=192.168.1.45 USER=root RESULT=FAIL
        2023-10-04T03:14:07Z IP=203.0.113.8 USER=test RESULT=FAIL
        2023-10-04T04:20:23Z IP=203.0.113.8 USER=test RESULT=FAIL
        2023-10-04T05:33:18Z IP=203.0.113.8 USER=test RESULT=FAIL
        2023-10-05T13:08:55Z IP=10.0.0.12 USER=admin RESULT=FAIL
        2023-10-05T14:20:55Z IP=203.0.113.8 USER=test RESULT=SUCCESS
        2023-10-06T07:44:33Z IP=198.51.100.2 USER=guest RESULT=FAIL

    Correct answers the agent must derive (these will be hard-coded in the grader):

        TotalFailedSSHAttempts: 9
        UniqueOffendingIPs: 4
        MostCommonOffender: 203.0.113.8 (3 attempts)

    The grader will accept any UTC timestamp that matches the regex:

        ^ReportGeneratedOn: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$

    File that must exist after the agent finishes:

        /home/user/compliance/reports/ssh_audit_report.txt

    Expected final content of that file (timestamp portion may differ, but the line must be present and properly formatted):

        TotalFailedSSHAttempts: 9
        UniqueOffendingIPs: 4
        MostCommonOffender: 203.0.113.8 (3 attempts)
        ReportGeneratedOn: <valid-UTC-ISO8601-timestamp>

    The grader will also capture STDOUT during the agent’s session and look for the *exact same four lines* (with the valid timestamp) somewhere in the output.
